root: ^Satan journeys alone through the vast emptiness of Hell, leaving Pandemonium and its debates behind.
^Ahead, the darkness thickens. A shape emerges — immense gates, wrought of burning adamant, barred with threefold brass, threefold iron, threefold rock.
^The first is a woman — beautiful above the waist, but below, a mass of coiling serpents. Hellhounds bay ceaselessly around her middle, retreating into her body and emerging again.
^The second is a shape — if shape it can be called — a darkness within darkness. It wears a crown, and in its skeletal hand it raises what might be a spear, or a scythe, or nothing at all.
^You shall not pass these gates, proud spirit. Turn back to your burning throne.
^The shapeless thing beside her says nothing, but the air grows cold. Colder than the lake of fire could ever burn.
^"Stand aside. I am Satan, Lord of this realm, and I bow to no gatekeeper."
^"Who are you, that dares bar my path? Speak, and speak quickly."
^Study the woman's face — there is something familiar there.

c-0: ^Satan draws himself to his full terrible height, wings spread wide, the old glory flickering about him like heat lightning.
^The shadow-king shifts, and where it moves, existence seems to thin. It raises its weapon-shape.
^Stay your hand, Death. He does not yet know.
^She turns to Satan, and her expression is strange — hurt, perhaps, or bitter amusement.
^You truly do not recognise me? Then let me remind you. I am Sin. I sprang from your head, fully formed, on the day you first conceived rebellion. I am your daughter.
c-1: ^The woman tilts her head. A sad smile crosses her beautiful, terrible face.
^You demand my name? You, who gave it to me? I am Sin — born from your mind on the very day you conceived your great rebellion. Before all the host of Heaven, I sprang from your brow.
c-2: ^Satan pauses. The woman's eyes — there is something in them. A light he recognises, though he cannot say from where.
^He studies the line of her jaw, the defiant tilt of her chin. It is like looking into a dark mirror.
^I am Sin. Your daughter. Born from your thoughts when you first dared to rebel. In Heaven, the angels recoiled from me — all except you. You alone did not look away.

revelation: ^The memory surfaces — unwanted, undeniable. In Heaven, before the war, a form erupting from his mind. The angels fleeing in horror. And he, alone, reaching toward the strange new creature.
^This is Death. Your son. Our son. He was born here, in this darkness, and the first thing he did was turn upon me. He knows nothing but hunger. He fears nothing — except, perhaps, you.
^Death shifts. The air crackles. Something like a growl emanates from the formless shape, though it has no throat to make it.
^We were set here as wardens of these gates. No soul may pass — by decree of the Almighty.
^"Decree? I defied His greatest decree when I raised war in Heaven. Open the gates."
^"Daughter... I did not know. Forgive the years of forgetting. But I must pass."

c-0: ^Sin flinches at the force in his voice. Death raises its weapon — but hesitates.
^The old authority. The voice that shook Heaven's foundations. Even here, it has power.
^You would walk into Chaos itself? Beyond these gates is nothing — no ground, no sky, no light. The formless void between Hell and the world above.
^Many have approached these gates. None have returned from what lies beyond.
c-1: ^Something shifts in Sin's expression. The hard wariness softens, just slightly.
^Forgive? There is nothing to forgive. I am what you made me, Father. I could no more hate you for it than a river could hate its source.
^She glances at Death, who stands motionless, unreadable.
^But these gates... beyond them lies Chaos. The unformed void. It is not a place meant for any being, fallen or otherwise.
c-2: ^Sin gestures toward the massive gates. Even closed, something seeps through — a sound like the roaring of an ocean with no shore.
^Chaos. The void between all worlds. Formless, violent, without law or reason. It is older than Heaven, older than Hell. Through it — if one survives — lies the new creation. The world of Man.
^God's newest toy. His replacement for us.

the_bargain: ^Satan looks at the gates. He can feel it now — the pull of the void beyond, like standing at the edge of an infinite cliff.
^I must reach the world above. God has made a new creation — beings called Man. If I can reach them, corrupt them, turn them as I turned the angels... then our defeat is not final.
^Sin considers this. She looks at Death, who tilts his crowned shadow in what might be a nod.
^If you succeed... if Man falls... then Death and I would have dominion beyond this gate. We could feed upon a whole world, not merely guard an empty threshold.
^"Open the gates. When I succeed, all the world shall be yours to roam."
^"I make no promises. But if the gates open, what comes after benefits us all."
^"You would bargain with your own father? Very well — name your price."

c-0: ^Sin smiles — a terrible, beautiful, hungry smile.
^She reaches for the gates. Where her hands touch the burning adamant, it hisses but does not harm her. She was made for this — the keeper of Hell's threshold.
^The bars draw back. Brass, iron, rock — all yield to her touch.
c-1: ^Sin studies him. The careful politician, even with his own daughter.
^No promises. How very like you, Father. But I will take the gamble. Better a chance at freedom than an eternity of guarding nothing.
^She turns to the gates and begins to work the locks.
c-2: ^Bargain? No. I ask no price. You are my father. If this venture brings Death and me a wider world... that is enough.
^But know this — once opened, these gates can never be closed again. What passes through, passes through forever.
^She lays her hands upon the gates.

the_opening: ^The sound is like nothing Satan has ever heard — a groan from the foundations of reality itself. The massive doors swing wide, and beyond them...

^Not darkness — darkness would be something. This is the absence of everything. A roaring, churning void where matter and un-matter collide, where hot and cold and wet and dry wage eternal war without victory.
^Satan stands at the threshold. Behind him, the fires of Hell. Before him, the abyss.
^Sin calls out over the roar of the void.
^Fly true, Father. And when you have ruined Man — remember your promise. Leave the gates open for us.
^Death says nothing. But for the first time, the shapeless horror steps aside.
^Satan spreads his wings — scorched, magnificent, defiant — and hurls himself into the void.
